Title: Advanced Permissions for Umbraco
Description: Fine-grained user group permission management for content, document types and the Library: explicit Allow/Deny entries with per-entry scope, tree inheritance, an All Users Group baseline, per-group control over which document and element types may be created, and full audit reasoning for every effective permission.

AuthorDetails.Name: Luuk Peters
AuthorDetails.Description: Umbraco developer building packages that extend the CMS with enterprise-grade features. Creator of Advanced Permissions for Umbraco.

Screenshots.Caption: Permissions Editor: browse the content tree and manage permission entries per user group.
Set Allow or Deny for each verb, with a configurable scope (This Node Only, This Node and Descendants, or Descendants Only).
Access Viewer: inspect effective permissions for any user or user group at any content node.
Full reasoning chain showing which user group contributed, from which node, and whether it was explicit or inherited.
The Users section gains four permission groups in the sidebar — Content, Document Type, Library and Library Element Type — each with a Permissions Editor and an Access Viewer.
In each user group's editor, the native Documents permissions panel is replaced with a link to the Content Permissions Editor.
